description: Acceptance of Critical-rated risk for cross-border data transfer non-compliance (Schrems II). After comprehensive legal analysis by external EU counsel (Fieldfisher LLP, engagement ref: FL-2025-VAC-0187), determined that current Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) and supplementary technical measures meet adequacy requirements under current enforcement guidance. However, residual risk remains due to ongoing regulatory uncertainty and potential legal challenges to the EU-US Data Privacy Framework.
compensating_controls: 1) Standard Contractual Clauses (2021 version) executed with all EU data processors. 2) Supplementary technical measures implemented: AES-256 encryption at rest and in transit, pseudonymization of personal data in analytics pipelines, EU data residency for primary processing (eu-west-1 Frankfurt). 3) Cyber liability insurance policy with Beazley (Policy: CL-2025-VAC-0042) covers regulatory fines and penalties up to $10M, with $2M retention. 4) Legal monitoring service engaged to track DPA enforcement actions and CJEU rulings. 5) Data Transfer Impact Assessment (DTIA) completed and documented for all transfer mechanisms.
board_notified: Yes - Board Risk Committee briefed 2025-03-25. Board resolution BR-2025-012 approving risk acceptance.
notes: Quarterly reviews completed: Q2 (2025-06-20), Q3 (2025-09-18), Q4 (2025-12-20). No material changes to regulatory landscape. EU-US DPF remains in effect. Insurance coverage renewed with same terms. Next Board review scheduled for Q1 2026 SGC meeting.
